name: Enable Compression
desc: Enables zlib compression for all subsequent packets. Sets the compression threshold — packets >= this size are compressed.

fields.name: compressionThreshold
fields.desc: Minimum packet size in bytes before compression is applied. -1 disables compression.

notes: After this packet, all future packets start with a VarInt prefix indicating the uncompressed length. If length=0, the data is uncompressed (smaller than threshold). If length>0, the remaining data is zlib-compressed and must be inflated.

implementation.overview: S03PacketEnableCompression is sent after login success and before any PLAY-state packets. It sets the compression threshold for the NetworkManager. Once compression is enabled, every packet's framed header includes a VarInt uncompressed-length prefix. Packets smaller than the threshold are sent uncompressed (length=0 prefix); larger packets are zlib-compressed. A compression threshold of -1 means 'never compress'. This packet matters for packet interception/tampering — man-in-the-middle tools need to decompress/recompress, and modules that modify raw packet bytes (like Proxy/VPN modules) must handle the compression layer correctly.

modules.name: Disabler (Compression Manipulation)
modules.purpose: Modify or cancel the compression threshold to manipulate how the server communicates, potentially causing desync between client and server packet framing.
modules.how_it_works: Some disabler modes detect when compression is enabled and respond by sending oversized packets that would trigger server-side compression errors. If the threshold is changed client-side but not server-side, the client will attempt to decompress uncompressed data (or vice versa), causing the server to kick with 'bad packet' errors — this is sometimes used to trigger anti-cheat false positives or to cause targeted lag spikes.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleEnableCompression(S03PacketEnableCompression) → NetworkManager.setCompressionTreshold(). This adds NettyCompressionDecoder and NettyCompressionEncoder to the Netty channel pipeline, which sit before the Minecraft packet codec.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Manipulating the compression threshold is not an anti-cheat concern — the server never reads the client's compression threshold. However, sending packets without proper compression framing after S03 will cause Netty decode errors on the server side, which look like 'bad packet' kicks.

modules.name: Packet Sniffer / MITM Preparation
modules.purpose: Track when compression is enabled so packet sniffing/capturing tools can correctly decompress subsequent packets for analysis.
modules.how_it_works: Packet sniffers that intercept raw Netty channel data need to know when compression kicks in because the packet format changes. Before S03: `[VarInt length][VarInt packetId][data]`. After S03: `[VarInt frameLength][VarInt uncompressedLength][compressed/uncompressed data]`. The sniffer module records the threshold value from this packet and adjusts its parsing accordingly.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetworkManager.setCompressionTreshold() — called from handleEnableCompression. This is a key NetworkManager state change.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: N/A — this is a client-side informational tool. The server is unaware of client-side sniffing.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client-side: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleEnableCompression(S03PacketEnableCompression) sets the NetworkManager's compression threshold. The vanilla threshold is typically 256 bytes (set in server.properties `network-compression-threshold`).

Server-side: The server sends this after S02PacketLoginSuccess if compression is enabled in server.properties. It's sent before any PLAY state packets, ensuring both client and server agree on compression settings before gameplay data flows.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: BlinkComponent exempts S03PacketEnableCompression from blink (it must be processed immediately).
Aqua 13: Backtrack module exempts this packet from its replay buffer.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific module targets S03 — it's processed through the vanilla NetHandlerLoginClient path with Jigsaw.onPacketRecieved hook.
